items.title: Barcelona in de achtervolging: 'Flick hunkert naar terugkeer Pedri'
items.summary: In de nieuwe aflevering van VI ZSM blikken Jos Boesveld en Jarno Verweij terug op de wedstrijd van Barcelona op bezoek bij Girona. Barcelona heeft maandagavond zijn vierde competitienederlaag van het seizoen geleden . Vlak voor tijd ging het voor de ploeg van Hansi Flick mis in de derby tegen Girona. Real Madrid gaat nu alleen aan kop in La Liga.

items.title: Why Benfica tie is pivotal for Arbeloa and Real Madrid
items.summary: After a difficult start as Real Madrid manager, Alvaro Arbeloa will know the importance of progressing past Jose Mourinho's Benfica in the Champions League play-off round.

items.title: Arbeloa kijkt uit naar clash met Mourinho: 'Ik denk niet dat hij me kan verrassen'
items.summary: Álvaro Arbeloa kijkt uit naar de ontmoeting met zijn oud-trainer José Mourinho. Dinsdag staat hij met Real Madrid tegenover Benfica in de tussenronde van de Champions League. Arbeloa meent het voordeel te hebben dat hij Mourinho door en door kent.

items.title: Henk de Jong prijst 'apart mannetje' Balk: 'Voor ons goud waard'
items.summary: SC Cambuur sleepte maandagavond drie punten uit het vuur bij Jong Ajax en dat was volgens trainer Henk de Jong voor een belangrijk deel te danken aan Remco Balk. De 24-jarige aanvaller was in de eerste helft verantwoordelijk voor het verdienen van een strafschop voor de Friezen.

items.title: Macclesfield FC's cup magic ends with an own goal, but with their legacy secure
items.summary: A second FA Cup shock always seemed unrealistic for tiny Macclesfield, but Monday's home defeat to Brentford does little to shake their legacy in this storied competition.

items.title: Real Madrid need reinvention as special circumstances are needed to rule Europe | Philipp Lahm
items.summary: Carlo Ancelotti and Zinedine Zidane’s impact is clear but where the Spanish giants go post-Xabi Alonso only Florentino Pérez knows All eyes are on Real Madrid again but in a different way than to which the 15-time Champions League winners are accustomed. Every fan expects Real to be among the best eight in Europe but they are in the playoffs for a second season in a row after a 4-2 defeat at Benfica in the final league fixture. Looking back, Carlo Ancelotti’s impact at Real is even clearer, as is the case with Zinedine Zidane, who won three successive Champions League titles with the club. Why were they suited to this club? Because they themselves once stood on the pitch alongside outstanding footballers. Ancelotti also played under Arrigo Sacchi at Milan while Zidane scored key goals in Champions League and World Cup finals. People with this aura are respected by the best. Continue reading...

items.title: ‘They were like bombs’: Helenio Herrera’s little white pills kept Inter’s players buzzing
items.summary: In an extract from his book, Richard Fitzpatrick reveals in the early 1960s the grand Italian club was equipped for doping like ‘a small hospital’ The quantity of drugs floating around the campus at Inter in the early 1960s meant the club was equipped like “a small hospital”, to borrow an expression used about the doping culture at Juventus in the 1990s. Inter’s coach Helenio Herrera – or “HH”, as he was known in the world of football – used the players on the youth team as “guinea pigs” for his drug experiments, according to Ferruccio Mazzola, who was on the books at Inter’s academy at the time (and a younger brother of Sandro Mazzola, one of the team’s star players). “I can describe the effects of those white tablets,” he wrote in a confessional memoir. He said he couldn’t sleep after taking HH’s pills. The hallucinations left him like a fish thrown up on the bank of a river. “I was shaking all over. I looked like an epileptic. I was scared. Also, the effect lasted for days and was followed by a sudden, tremendous tiredness.” Continue reading...

items.title: Porteous on playing with Son, facing Messi & his World Cup dream
items.summary: Ryan Porteous is determined to return to the Scotland squad for this summer's World Cup in the USA.

items.title: Griekenland als rivaal? AZ moet 'coëfficiëntenschade' beperken
items.summary: Na de bijltjesdagen van eind januari is AZ de enige Nederlandse club onder de 72 overgebleven Europese deelnemers. De Alkmaarders moeten de wond die is ontstaan op de coëfficiëntenlijst proberen te stelpen. De stand van zaken bij de start van de tussenronde.

items.title: Oekraïne baalt van 'verwoestend nieuws' Zinchenko en 'angstaanjagende hersteltijd'
items.summary: Niet alleen in Amsterdam, maar ook in Oekraïne is de blessure van Oleksandr Zinchenko hard aangekomen. Maandag werd bekend dat de multifunctionele verdediger er de rest van het seizoen uit ligt . Daardoor mist hij ook de voor Oekraïne cruciale WK-play-offs en – indien geplaatst – misschien het toernooi zelf ook wel.

items.title: Is facing a Champions League play-off really that bad?
items.summary: The Champions League returns this week and some of Europe's biggest teams will be back in action a lot earlier than they would have hoped.

items.title: Fury: Usyk will beg me for a trilogy! 'Without belts, I'm still the man'
items.summary: Tyson Fury believes he will set a third fight with Oleksandr Usyk before the year is out.

items.title: 'We really messed up' - does European football need more balance?
items.summary: One set of clubs dominate the Champions League, another win their domestic title every year but cannot compete - does European football have a problem?

items.title: Button: Hamilton has 'perfect opportunity' to turn Ferrari story around
items.summary: The 2026 Formula 1 season provides Lewis Hamilton with the "perfect opportunity to turn it around" at Ferrari, according to his former team-mate Jenson Button.

items.title: Quebecois looks to get off the mark over fences at Newbury
items.summary: Quebecois faces Personal Ambition and Old Cowboy in Tuesday's feature from Newbury - live on Sky Sports Racing.

items.title: FA Cup: Wrexham to host Chelsea in fifth round
items.summary: Wrexham will host Chelsea in the fifth round of the FA Cup.

items.title: Luis Enrique slams 'worthless' Dembélé PSG take
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain coach Luis Enrique said he will not allow any player to be bigger than the club ahead of Tuesday's Champions League knockout phase playoff first leg against AS Monaco.

items.title: Own goal ends dream FA Cup run for Macclesfield
items.summary: Macclesfield were unable to recreate the magic of last month's FA Cup win over Crystal Palace, with a Sam Heathcote own goal sending Brentford through to the fifth round after a spirited contest at Moss Rose.

items.title: Klopp's agent: Man Utd, Chelsea made approach
items.summary: Jurgen Klopp's agent Marc Kosicke said his client had his pick of teams to manage after leaving Liverpool, including approaches from both the United States and England to take over as coach of their respective national teams.

items.title: ‘It fills my heart’: Andrews lauds Macclesfield for their dream FA Cup adventure
items.summary: Brentford coach praises non-league side’s advances Manager John Rooney proud of Macclesfield’s efforts Keith Andrews made a beeline for the Macclesfield dressing room after Brentford’s fourth-round FA Cup victory at Moss Rose and told John Rooney and his players they had been immense against his Premier League charges. The Brentford head coach said footballing journeys like the one Macclesfield have enjoyed since their rebirth “fills my heart” as he waxed lyrical about the true test his team were given by the National League North outfit. Continue reading...
